reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully encodes the informal claim. The hypotheses `Odd p`, `Nat.Prime p`, and `p ≡ 2 [MOD 3]` correspond directly to "odd prime with p ≡ 2 (mod 3)". The permutation π(x) ≡ x³ (mod p) is encoded as `P : Equiv.Perm (Fin p)` with `P i = i * i * i`, since multiplication in `Fin p` is done modulo p, so `i * i * i` equals i³ mod p. The hypothesis p ≡ 2 (mod 3) ensures the cube map is bijective on Fin p, so such P exists and is unique. For `Perm (Fin p)`, `signAux P` equals `Equiv.Perm.sign P` (both are multiplicative homomorphisms agreeing on generators: 1 on identity and -1 on swaps), so `signAux P = 1` is precisely the condition that P is an even permutation. The conclusion `p ≡ 3 [MOD 4]` matches p ≡ 3 (mod 4). All components align with the informal statement.
